schemaCaptureBehaviorHeroDescription: Reading session and fragment clipping behaviors.

schemaCaptureBehaviorModifierConflictSys: {label} may conflict with system, browser, or page shortcuts. If it is unstable, use Shift.
schemaCaptureBehaviorSidebarHighlightsNo: Saved highlights work best with the Obsidian plugin Sidebar Highlights.

schemaCaptureSourcesAttachmentGuidancePr: Use these attachment settings with the Obsidian plugin
schemaCaptureSourcesAttachmentGuidanceSu: and keep the plugin configuration in Obsidian aligned with these templates.
schemaCaptureSourcesAutoPauseDescription: When enabled, the current video pauses while you edit a timestamp note in Video Mode.
schemaCaptureSourcesMarkdownUrlDescripti: Leave blank to use the default format, or provide a Markdown link template for the current export flow.
schemaCaptureSourcesScreenshotFilenameDe: For example `file-${date:{momentJsFormat:'YYYYMMDDHHmmssSSS'}}.jpg`. This controls screenshot filenames.
schemaCaptureSourcesScreenshotLocationDe: For example `./assets/${noteFileName}`. This controls where screenshot attachments are stored.
schemaCaptureSourcesSupportedPlatformsDe: Supported AI websites are shown here so users can quickly verify coverage.
schemaCaptureSourcesUserDisplayNameDescr: Control the display name used for user messages in exported AI conversations.
schemaCaptureSourcesVideoEntryBehaviorDe: Control the note entry on video sites and whether playback pauses while timestamp notes are edited.
schemaCaptureSourcesVideoNoteButtonLabel: Show note button on video sites
schemaCaptureSourcesVideoPromptEntryDesc: Configure the note entry shown in supported video players.
schemaCaptureSourcesVideoPromptGreenDotD: mean a screenshot is already attached.
schemaCaptureSourcesVideoPromptGreyDotDe: mean no screenshot has been saved for that timestamp yet.
schemaCaptureSourcesVideoPromptToggleHin: Click a dot to change the screenshot state.
schemaExperimentalHeroDescription: Shared service settings and planned experimental features.
schemaExperimentalPageSummaryToggleDescr: Insert the summary at the beginning of the saved document.
schemaExperimentalPageSummaryToggleTitle: Generate an AI summary when saving a page
schemaExperimentalReadingOverlayToggleDe: Display the current page summary in the reading overlay window.

schemaMaintenanceConfigurationDiagnosisD: Check connections, templates, routing, and capture settings.
schemaMaintenanceConfigurationTransferDe: Copy and import configuration between browsers.
schemaMaintenanceDiagnosisResultLog:
Diagnosis Results
========
✅ Default vault HTTPS connection is healthy
✅ At least one additional vault is enabled
✅ Routing rule priorities do not conflict
✅ article / clipper / video / ai_chat YAML configuration all parses successfully
⚠️ fragment.contextLength = 200 is reasonable, but monitor performance in heavier Reader Mode sessions
ℹ️ AI page summaries, Reader Mode top summaries, and subtitle translation are still planned and were not included in this connectivity check
ℹ️ video.floatingPromptEnabled controls whether the note button appears in the video-site control bar
schemaMaintenanceDiagnosisScopeDescripti: REST API, path templates, domain mappings, multi-vault routing, fragment context settings, and video prompts should all appear in the report.
schemaMaintenanceTransferHelperDescripti: Copy exports the current configuration. Import reads configuration JSON from the clipboard, validates it, and saves it.
schemaOutputHeroDescription: Templates, domain naming, and YAML fields.
schemaOutputPathTemplatesDescription: Configure storage paths for each export flow.
schemaOutputTemplateHelperText: Focus a path field above, then click a token below to insert it quickly.
schemaOutputYamlHelperText: Use the structured editor above as the single place to manage fields, content-type switches, custom fields, and domain overrides.
schemaOverviewHeroDescription: Usage overview, interface language, and privacy controls.
schemaOverviewLanguageRowDescription: Switching language reloads the formal settings copy.
schemaPreviewTaskSuccessDislikeToastDeta: Continue the discussion on Reddit or file an issue on GitHub.
schemaPreviewTaskSuccessLikeToastDetail: If you want to help more people discover Zendio, feel free to leave a review.
schemaPreviewVideoCaptureTwoSummary: "What creates understanding is the judgment you make while watching."
schemaRendererHighlightExampleSuffix: so they are easy to revisit.
schemaResourceChangelogDescription: This modal highlights the latest shipped updates from the project changelog.
schemaResourceChangelogV010Bullet1: Released the base webpage clipping flow.
schemaResourceChangelogV010Bullet3: Added basic path templates and domain mappings.
schemaResourceChangelogV010Bullet5: Added early AI chat export support.
schemaResourceChangelogV010Summary: Amid a stressful recruiting season, the first version was vibe-coded in two hours.
schemaResourceChangelogV020Bullet1: Rebuilt Options as a new settings center for overview, language, privacy, storage, capture, output, and maintenance workflows.
schemaResourceChangelogV020Bullet10: Officially supports 12 interface languages across the main Options flows.
schemaResourceChangelogV020Bullet2: Added multi-vault management and smart routing by domain, keyword, or URL Pattern.
schemaResourceChangelogV020Bullet3: Improved Obsidian writes with HTTPS / HTTP dual endpoints, connection tests, and REST API fallback.
schemaResourceChangelogV020Bullet4: Added Chrome / Chromium local Vault folder writes so authorized browsers can write by template path and fall back to REST API when permission or writing fails.
schemaResourceChangelogV020Bullet5: Clipper, Reader, and Video panels can now switch save targets and preview output paths across Vault, local folder, or download destinations.
schemaResourceChangelogV020Bullet6: Enhanced fragment clipping and reading mode with context capture, footnote format, shortcuts, highlight themes, and reading export modes.
schemaResourceChangelogV020Bullet7: Added video notes for YouTube / Bilibili timestamps, subtitle or comment fragment capture, and editable annotations.
schemaResourceChangelogV020Bullet8: Expanded AI chat export for ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Copilot, Tongyi, DeepSeek, Kimi, Doubao, Monica, Perplexity, and more.
schemaResourceChangelogV020Bullet9: Added structured YAML configuration, path templates, domain mappings, configuration migration, and diagnostics/repair tools.
schemaResourceChangelogV020Summary: It was said to be almost done, then took another two or three months - the noisy age keeps busy making things.
schemaResourceChangelogV021Bullet1: AI chat export stability has been improved.
schemaResourceChangelogV021Bullet2: AI chat page detection is stronger, reducing export failures, missing content, and incorrect role detection.
schemaResourceChangelogV021Bullet3: Conversation-scope detection is more reliable when page labels, role markers, or candidate content change, so exports are less likely to capture the wrong content.
schemaResourceChangelogV021Bullet4: The AI platform support list in Options now follows actual export capability, avoiding mismatches between displayed support and real behavior.
schemaResourceChangelogV021Bullet5: Some performance behavior was optimized, and Chrome / Firefox pre-release checks were completed.
schemaResourceChangelogV021Summary: Graduation arrived with deadline chaos alongside it.
schemaResourceContactDescription: If you appreciate this project or want to connect, visit the author's website, Reddit, Github, or email.
schemaResourceContactWechatNote: Please mention where you came from.
schemaResourceDataUsageAnonymousUsageBod: Usage Dashboard totals come from local counters. Optional GA4 usage analytics is separate, off by default, and sends only low-cardinality event names and sanitized buckets after you opt in.
schemaResourceDataUsageConfigMigrationBo: Copy/import tools read configuration JSON from the clipboard, validate it locally, and save the merged result.
schemaResourceDataUsageDescription: Understand local usage counters, optional GA4 analytics/error reporting, and configuration transfer data boundaries.
schemaResourceOnboardingDescription: Connect Obsidian first, then configure vault routing, capture behavior, and output rules from one setup guide.
schemaResourcePluginSetupChecklist1: Obsidian is running and Local REST API is enabled.
schemaResourcePluginSetupChecklist2: HTTPS and HTTP endpoints match the plugin settings.
schemaResourcePluginSetupChecklist4: API key was copied without extra spaces.
schemaResourcePluginSetupChecklist5: The Storage connection test returns success.
schemaResourcePluginSetupDescription: Configure Obsidian Local REST API before editing advanced storage rules.
schemaResourcePluginSetupStep1: Install and enable Obsidian Local REST API in Community Plugins.
schemaResourcePluginSetupStep2: Open the plugin settings and confirm both HTTPS and HTTP endpoints.
schemaResourcePluginSetupStep3: Copy the vault name and API key from Obsidian.
schemaResourcePluginSetupStep4: Return to Storage and fill the default vault row first.
schemaResourcePluginSetupStep5: Run the connection test before saving more routing rules.
schemaResourcePrivacyLocalConfigBody: Vault names, API endpoints, tokens, routing rules, templates, local-folder handles, draft metadata, and imported configuration stay in the current browser profile unless you export or clear them yourself.
schemaResourcePrivacyPolicyChoicesBody: You can disable analytics and error diagnostics independently, clear analytics data, edit or delete saved vault settings, revoke REST API tokens, remove local folder permissions, clear browser extension data, or uninstall Zendio. You can also contact the maintainer to ask about privacy, access, correction, or deletion requests.
schemaResourcePrivacyPolicyContactBody: For privacy questions or requests, contact us by email or open a GitHub issue.
schemaResourcePrivacyPolicyDescription: Review what Zendio processes locally, what optional telemetry can send, and how to control or delete data.
schemaResourcePrivacyPolicyEffectiveBody: This Privacy Policy is effective June 20, 2026 and applies to Zendio for Chrome, Chromium-based browsers, and Firefox builds where available.
schemaResourcePrivacyPolicyLocalDataBody: To provide the features you request, Zendio may process and store limited data in browser extension storage, extension IndexedDB, memory, or the destination you choose.
schemaResourcePrivacyPolicyLocalDataBull: Clipped text, article metadata, selected fragments, reading notes, video timestamps, screenshot references, and AI chat export structure are processed when you trigger those workflows.
Settings such as vault names, route rules, path templates, YAML rules, interface preferences, REST API URLs, and API tokens are stored locally until you change, clear, or uninstall the extension.

Reader and video draft recovery stores metadata for recent unfinished work. The open-source default keeps the most recent 48 hours, up to 5 page identities, and up to 20 recoverable items per page; video screenshot bytes are stored in an extension IndexedDB Blob cache, not in telemetry.
When you choose a local vault folder in Chromium, the browser gives Zendio access only to the folder handle you authorize; Zendio does not enumerate unrelated folders.
schemaResourcePrivacyPolicyLocalFirstBod: Core clipping, parsing, previewing, draft recovery, configuration, and Obsidian writing flows are designed to run locally in your browser, your authorized folder, or your local Obsidian environment. Zendio does not send page content to the developer by default.
schemaResourcePrivacyPolicyNotCollectedB: Zendio telemetry is not a content analytics system. The following data is not sent to GA4 or the developer by telemetry events.
Page body, clipped text, article text, AI chat content, reading highlight text, video note text, comments, screenshots, image bytes, data URLs, or exported Markdown content.
Personal identity information unless you choose to contact the developer and provide it yourself.
Passwords, API tokens, cookies, auth headers, Obsidian REST API keys, browser credentials, or other secrets.
Full URLs, URL query strings, page titles, vault names, file names, local folder names, local paths, route patterns, YAML field values, or template output.
schemaResourcePrivacyPolicyObsidianBody: If you configure Obsidian Local REST API, Zendio sends local requests only to the HTTP or HTTPS endpoint you provide. The API token stays in extension storage and can be replaced, revoked in Obsidian, or cleared from Zendio settings.
schemaResourcePrivacyPolicyRetentionBody: Local settings remain until you change them, clear extension data, or uninstall Zendio. Temporary clip data is released after the requested workflow finishes. Draft recovery and screenshot cache follow the local retention limits described above. GA4 event data, if you opt in, follows the maintainer policy currently configured for 14 months. You can clear analytics data from Options, revoke Obsidian tokens in Obsidian, clear local folder access in Zendio, or remove extension data from the browser.
schemaResourcePrivacyPolicyScopeBody: Zendio helps you save selected web content, reading highlights, video timestamp notes, screenshots, and AI chat exports into destinations you configure, such as Obsidian vaults, local folders, downloads, or a local Obsidian REST API endpoint.
schemaResourcePrivacyPolicySecurityBody: Zendio follows a least-permission design, uses browser extension storage and File System Access permission boundaries, rejects unsafe relative paths, keeps server-only analytics secrets out of client builds, and uses release-surface audits to block secret or direct telemetry endpoint leakage.
schemaResourcePrivacyPolicySharingBody: Zendio does not sell, rent, or share your personal data for advertising. Optional telemetry, if enabled, is shared with Google Analytics 4 through the developer proxy only for product reliability and improvement. If a future feature expands data sharing, Zendio should disclose it before enabling that behavior.
schemaResourcePrivacyPolicyTelemetryBody: Analytics and error diagnostics are off unless you enable them. You can change both choices in the first-run page or Options privacy settings, and those settings are saved automatically.
schemaResourcePrivacyPolicyTelemetryBull: Usage analytics may send low-cardinality product events such as feature usage, onboarding progress, save outcomes, and version-level signals.
Error diagnostics may send sanitized extension error events, browser family, extension version, timestamp, and failure category.
Local analytics client and session identifiers are stored in extension storage for consented telemetry stability. They are removed when you clear analytics data or extension data.
When enabled, events go through a developer-controlled proxy to Google Analytics 4. The extension does not contain server-side GA secrets and does not call Google Measurement Protocol directly.
schemaResourcePrivacyPolicyUpdatesBody: This policy may be updated when Zendio changes features, data flows, third-party services, or legal requirements. Material changes should be reflected in the project documentation, release notes, or extension UI before or when the new behavior ships.
schemaResourceSuggestionsDescription: This project is developed by a solo author using Codex. If you run into any errors or have product suggestions, please reach out through
schemaResourceSuggestionsXiaohongshuQrCa: Scan with Xiaohongshu to join the group
schemaResourceSupportAfdianDescription: Scan the WeChat reward code to support the project.
schemaResourceSupportDescription: Support the project through the available public channels.
schemaResourceTermsAcceptanceBody: By installing, enabling, or using Zendio, you agree to use the extension under these terms. If you do not agree, disable or uninstall the extension.
schemaResourceTermsAvailabilityBody: Zendio is provided as an evolving personal productivity tool. Features may change, break, be removed, or depend on browser, website, Obsidian, or platform behavior outside the developer's control.
schemaResourceTermsChangesBody: You may stop using Zendio at any time by disabling or uninstalling it. The developer may update these terms when product behavior, distribution channels, or legal requirements change. Continued use after an update means you accept the updated terms.
schemaResourceTermsContactBody: For questions about these terms, contact us by email or open a GitHub issue.
schemaResourceTermsDescription: Review the responsibilities, service boundaries, and local-first expectations for using Zendio.
schemaResourceTermsEffectiveBody: These Terms of Use are effective June 20, 2026 and apply to your installation and use of Zendio.
schemaResourceTermsLiabilityBody: Zendio is provided as is, without warranties of uninterrupted availability, perfect accuracy, or fitness for a particular purpose. To the maximum extent allowed by law, the developer is not liable for lost data, failed exports, service interruptions, site changes, token misuse, or decisions made based on exported content.
schemaResourceTermsLiabilityTitle: No warranty and limitation of liability
schemaResourceTermsLocalFirstBody: Zendio is designed to save content into destinations you configure, such as Obsidian vaults, local folders, downloads, or local Obsidian REST API endpoints. You control the destinations and credentials you authorize.
schemaResourceTermsPrivacyBody: Zendio is local-first by default. Optional analytics and error diagnostics can be enabled or disabled independently. The Privacy Policy explains what data is processed locally, what telemetry may send when enabled, and how to clear or revoke data.
schemaResourceTermsProductBody: Zendio is a browser extension for saving user-selected web content, reading notes, video timestamp notes, screenshots, and AI chat exports into user-configured personal knowledge workflows.
schemaResourceTermsThirdPartyBody: Zendio may interact with your browser, Obsidian, Obsidian Local REST API, local folders, downloads, websites you visit, and optional telemetry infrastructure. Those third-party services are governed by their own terms and privacy practices.
schemaResourceTermsUserResponsibilityBod: You are responsible for how you use Zendio and for the content, accounts, sites, and destinations involved in your workflows.
schemaResourceTermsUserResponsibilityBul: Make sure you have the right to save, export, quote, or reuse the content you clip or annotate.
Verify vault paths, local folder permissions, routing rules, templates, YAML fields, and exported files before relying on them.
Protect your Obsidian REST API token, browser profile, vault folders, and any exported files or shared notes.
Follow the terms of websites, AI services, video platforms, Obsidian plugins, and browser stores that you use with Zendio.
schemaRuntimeClipperDescription: Dialog users see after selecting text on a webpage.
schemaRuntimeClipperSelectedText: What matters most is not the information itself, but the links between that information and what you already know. Only when those links are written down can your future self re-enter the original train of thought.
schemaRuntimeReaderDescription: Floating reader panel with real highlight lists and inline note editing.
schemaRuntimeReaderHighlightOneComment: This works well near the top of the article as the lead note for the Reader Mode export.
schemaRuntimeReaderHighlightOneExcerpt: What matters most is not the information itself, but the links between that information and what you already know.
schemaRuntimeReaderHighlightThreeDraft: Add a sentence here explaining why the judgment belongs in Reader Mode instead of waiting until later in Obsidian.
schemaRuntimeReaderHighlightThreeFullTex: A highlight is only an anchor; what really helps memory is the judgment you write beside it. The value of Reader Mode is turning that step from a mental action into structured output.
schemaRuntimeReaderHighlightTwoComment: Add an example here to explain why saving a link alone is not enough.
schemaRuntimeReaderHighlightTwoExcerpt: The value of information is not that it was stored, but that it can be understood again in the future.
schemaRuntimeTaskSuccessDescription: Success prompt shown after a save finishes.
schemaRuntimeTaskSuccessStatusDetail: Content was saved using the current vault routing, and classification is complete.
schemaRuntimeVideoCaptureOneComment: This is the first explicit definition of the core concept. Cross-reference it with the article version later.
schemaRuntimeVideoCaptureThreeDraft: Expand the case here and include that counterexample from the comments before saving.
schemaRuntimeVideoCaptureTwoComment: This subtitle fragment works well as a standalone quote with a follow-up note.
schemaRuntimeVideoCaptureTwoFullText: What creates understanding is the judgment you make while watching. The timestamp only helps you return to the moment; the explanation still comes from your note and the surrounding context.
schemaRuntimeVideoDescription: Video note panel built around timestamps, captured fragments, and comments.
schemaStorageConnectionNotRun: Connection test has not run yet.
schemaStorageHeroDescription: Manage vault connections and routing rules.
schemaStorageLocalFolderAuthorizeWarning: Could not authorize a local folder. Chromium supports this feature. Until permission is granted, Zendio continues to use the REST API.
schemaStorageLocalFolderReauthorizeBody: Chrome reset the local-folder permission for "{vaultName}". Click the folder again and allow read/write access in the browser prompt. Until then, sending falls back to the REST API.
schemaStorageLocalFolderReauthorizeFallb: Chrome could not restore this local-folder permission right now. Re-select the folder or continue with the REST API.
schemaStorageLocalFolderRecommendation: Local Folder is recommended first. REST API access is provided by the Obsidian plugin Local REST API with MCP.
schemaStorageNoEnabledVaults: No enabled vaults are available for testing.
schemaStorageVaultListDescription: The first row is the default vault. Remaining rows are additional vaults.
